talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: Dana-Farber Cancer Institute / Harvard Medical School
talk_metadata.talk_title: Mechanisms of response and resistance to the DLL3 bispecific T‑cell engager tarlatamab in small‑cell lung cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we really need larger numbers"
Speaker said: "we don't know at what level that is"
Speaker said: "we have one case, so we think it's less frequent where there's isolated DLL3 loss"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Subtype plasticity from ASCL1 to neuroD1 leading to DLL3 down‑regulation
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: we saw neuroD1 turn on and DLL3 go down at acquired resistance, but we don't yet know the exact mechanism
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: 80% of ASCL1 patients responded, but neuroD1 patients responded poorly despite high DLL3

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: DLL3‑mediated T‑cell killing in ASCL1‑high SCLC
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolled all comers without prospective molecular subtype selection (ASCL1 vs neuroD1 vs POU2F3)
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial ctDNA or plasma monitoring of subtype switching beyond the limited plasma ChIP‑seq snapshots
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Sample size small for subgroup analyses; underpowered to detect subtype‑specific efficacy

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Efficacy depends on DLL3 expression; neuroD1‑driven subtype switch and DLL3 loss render the drug ineffective
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad second‑line indication without biomarker‑driven patient selection dilutes signal

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that neuroD1 up‑regulation correlates with resistance, then concluded that DLL3 expression alone predicts response – ignoring the neuroD1‑driven escape

key_data_gaps: Limited longitudinal biomarker tracking of subtype switching

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a rapid plasma‑based assay (e.g., APEX) to assign ASCL1 vs neuroD1 subtype before tarlatamab initiation
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "the signatures associated with response were related to many different genes expressed in the ASCL1 subtype"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate cfChIP‑seq and RNA inference to deliver real‑time subtype scores for patient selection

crispro_opportunity.description: Target neuroD1‑driven transcriptional program (e.g., with BET or CDK7 inhibitors) to prevent DLL3 loss
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed that neuroD1 overexpression caused early resistance in mouse models
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict neuroD1‑dependent regulatory networks and suggest combination partners

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine tarlatamab with a GABA‑receptor antagonist or depolarization blocker to sustain DLL3 expression
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that GABAergic signaling was a major driver of depolarization and resistance
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model synergistic effects of T‑cell engagers with neuro‑modulatory agents

cited_competitors.name: Amgen sotorasib
cited_competitors.context: mentioned as an example of a targeted therapy that was unavailable for the namesake patient

unresolved_questions: What molecular mechanism links neuroD1 activation to DLL3 transcriptional silencing?
Can DLL3 expression be re‑induced after neuroD1‑driven resistance?
What is the timing of subtype switching during therapy?

assets_to_track: DLL3 bispecific T‑cell engagers from other companies

talk_metadata.talk_title: Surface‑targeting ADCs and emerging therapeutic strategies in small‑cell lung cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the first time I saw this data, it actually gave me goosebumps"
Speaker said: "we don't have any data so far on that, but I think it's a good question"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Low SLFN11 expression predicts reduced DNA‑damage response to Top1 payloads
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Some patients had dramatic responses while others did not, without clear biomarker explanation

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: CES6 surface antigen targeting with Top1‑linked payload
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolled all‑comer SCLC patients without SLFN11 or CES6 expression stratification
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: SLFN11 status and quantitative CES6 IHC
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small cohort; response rates reported without a control arm

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies on a single surface antigen; loss or low expression of CES6 would abrogate activity
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: ADC conjugation yields can be variable, affecting COGS
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Developed as second‑line monotherapy; no biomarker‑driven patient selection

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: B7H3 expression is heterogeneous across subtypes, especially low in POU2F3 tumors
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Potential off‑target toxicity due to B7H3 expression on normal tissues

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker highlighted high response rates and rapid symptom relief, yet admitted lack of biomarker data and limited OS follow‑up

key_data_gaps: No randomized comparison to standard chemotherapy
Long‑term safety and durability not reported

crispro_opportunity.description: Implement SLFN11 IHC or RNA assay to select patients most likely to benefit from Top1‑payload ADCs
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that SLFN11 has been used in several trials to identify patients for specific therapeutics
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate SLFN11 expression with tumor genomics to generate a predictive score for ADC response

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine CES6 ADC with ATR inhibitor to overcome resistance in SLFN11‑low tumors
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker mentioned data showing ATR inhibition may enhance response in low‑SLFN11 settings
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model synthetic lethality between DNA‑damage payloads and ATR blockade

crispro_opportunity.description: Design a biomarker‑driven basket trial that enrolls patients based on ASCL1 vs neuroD1 subtype and surface antigen expression
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker discussed subtype‑specific expression of DLL3, CES6, and B7H3
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can provide real‑time subtype classification from cfRNA to guide trial enrollment

cited_competitors.name: Amgen (tarlatamab)
cited_competitors.context: mentioned as the most advanced DLL3‑targeted bispecific T‑cell engager

cited_competitors.name: Roche (B7H3 ADCs)
cited_competitors.context: referenced as another surface‑targeting ADC in development

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal sequencing of ADCs with existing chemotherapy or immunotherapy?
How does CES6 expression change after prior DLL3‑targeted therapy?
Can SLFN11 be pharmacologically up‑regulated to sensitize tumors to ADCs?

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: Harvard Medical School / Brigham and Women's Hospital
talk_metadata.talk_title: Neural circuitry drives small‑cell lung cancer growth in brain metastases and primary lung
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we don't know at what level that is" regarding DLL3 silencing
Speaker said: "we have not specifically looked at that so far, but I think that's an interesting question" about Z6 cleavage

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Depolarization via GABAergic signaling in SCLC cells with high intracellular chloride

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Findings rely on neuronal circuitry; no drug platform disclosed
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Translating optogenetic or neuronal modulation to patients is currently infeasible

key_data_gaps: No in‑human validation of levetiracetam efficacy
Lack of pharmacodynamic biomarkers linking neuronal activity to tumor growth in patients

crispro_opportunity.description: Test levetiracetam (or other anti‑epileptics) in combination with DLL3‑targeted therapies to block activity‑mediated resistance
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed that levetiracetam dramatically reduced tumor burden in mouse models
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model synergistic effects of neuronal activity inhibitors with T‑cell engagers or ADCs

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a plasma‑based assay to detect GABAergic signaling signatures as a predictive biomarker for activity‑driven growth
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker demonstrated that GABAergic depolarization drives proliferation in vitro and in vivo
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate cfRNA/neurotransmitter‑related gene signatures to identify patients likely to benefit from neuronal blockade

cited_competitors.name: Levetiracetam (Keppra)
cited_competitors.context: used experimentally to inhibit neuronal activity in mouse models

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal dosing schedule of levetiracetam to achieve tumor suppression without CNS side effects?
Do peripheral nerve fibers in the lung microenvironment similarly drive SCLC growth in patients?
Can GABAergic signaling biomarkers be detected in patient plasma or CSF?

companies_to_monitor: Companies developing CNS‑active repurposed oncology agents
